Hi, I'm
Aleena Iraqui 👋

🌏 Bombay → Boston

MS Bioinformatics student at Northeastern University. I bridge wet lab biochemistry with computational pipelines to tackle genomic, structural, and clinical problems. Currently working at the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard.

@ Broad Institute @ Northeastern genomics ML for health structural biology vibe coder
LinkedIn GitHub

where I've been

Experience

Visiting Graduate Student May 2026 – Present
Machine Learning for Health (ML4H) Group · Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard, Cambridge MA
  • Applying causal inference methods (IPTW, competing risks, Cox models) to emulate a clinical trial on dementia onset using electronic health records
Graduate Research Assistant Jan 2026 – Present
Loring Lab · Northeastern University, Bouvé College of Health Sciences
  • Studying the C-terminal of α7 nicotinic acetylcholine receptors and chaperone proteins using combined computational and experimental approaches
Intern, Chemotherapy Department May 2024 – June 2024
Haffkine Institute for Training, Research and Testing · Mumbai, India
  • Applied molecular docking (AutoDock Vina, Schrödinger) and protein-ligand modelling (Discovery Studio Visualizer)
  • Mined data from PDB, DrugBank, PubChem, admetSAR, SwissADME, and ProTox
  • Docked BIBR1532 telomerase inhibitor to TERT using AutoDock Vina to map binding interactions
  • Hands-on training in HPLC, dissolution testing, and peptide synthesis
Intern, Department of Biochemistry May 2023
Tata Memorial Hospital · Mumbai, India
  • Observed clinical diagnostic workflows on instruments including Beckman Coulter, Cobas 6000, Alinity, Osmometer, and ELISA
  • Followed SOPs for sample handling, record-keeping, and patient data entry while maintaining confidentiality and safety standards
things I've built

Projects and Hackathons

🥇 1st Place
FoldForward
An agentic AI pipeline that converts scientific hypotheses into fully validated, execution-ready wet lab experiment plans. Compressed weeks of experiment planning into minutes using ESMFold for 3D structure prediction with pLDDT-based confidence scoring.
5th Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon · April 2026
🥇 Winner
Variant Pipeline
Multi-stage variant filtering pipeline (QC, population frequency, functional impact) that identified 1 pathogenic SCN1A variant from 750,000 candidates in the 1000 Genomes Project, establishing a Dravet Syndrome diagnosis via ClinVar, gnomAD, and OMIM.
GBBA BioHack 2026 · January 2026
🏅 Broadie's Choice Award
Lab Inventory AI
Developed an agentic lab inventory management system that uses ML vision and AI to automate tracking and management of laboratory supplies.
BroadHacks 2026 · Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard · June 2026
🏅 Top 5 Finalist
GenoInsight
An AI platform linking genomic variant discovery to clinical action. Built three integrated modules: AI Variant Hunter, Clinical Translation Engine, and Patient Dashboard. Reduced variant interpretation time from days to minutes.
Convergence 2026 Hackathon · January 2026
🧪 Drug Discovery
FoldForward: AI Drug Discovery Pipeline
Led a GPU-accelerated AI pipeline for Alzheimer's drug discovery, integrating ESMFold, Boltz-2, and AutoDock Vina. Identified Tropisetron as a drug repurposing candidate targeting α7 nAChR.
Sundai Club Hackathon
🔬 Research Project
PRIM-seq Pipeline
Genome-wide RNA-protein interaction mapping via PRIM-seq on HPC cluster using SLURM. Full pipeline: QC (cutadapt, fastp), alignment (BWA), chimeric read ID (SAMtools, BEDtools), with Chi-square/FDR validation.
Oct 2025 – Dec 2025 · Boston, MA
peer review incoming
